company_profile.name: OmniAb, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: OmniAb, Inc., a biotechnology company, licenses discovery research technology to pharmaceutical and biotech companies, and academic institutions to enable the discovery of therapeutics in the United States, Europe, Japan, China, and Canada. The company's technology platform creates and screens diverse antibody repertoires to identify optimal antibodies and other target-binding proteins for partners' drug development efforts. Its OmniAb platform's Biological Intelligence powers the immune systems of its proprietary and engineered transgenic animals to create optimized antibody candidates for human therapeutics. The company offers animal-based technologies include OmniRat, OmniChicken, and OmniMouse, which have been genetically modified to generate antibodies with human sequences; OmniFlic, a bispecific rat and OmniClic, a bispecific chicken are designed for discovery of bispecific antibody applications; OmniTaur, which provides cow-inspired antibodies with unique structural characteristics for challenging targets; OmnidAb, an in vivo platform for the discovery of single-domain antibodies based upon a human VH scaffold; and OmniDeep, a suite of in silico, an artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning tools for therapeutic discovery and optimization through various technologies and capabilities. It has an agreement with mAbsolve Ltd. for its Fc-silencing platform technology to incorporate the STR technology into antibodies that have been generated using OmniAb's antibody discovery platform. OmniAb, Inc. was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in Emeryville, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
This biotech company licenses its antibody discovery platform to pharma, biotech, and academic partners across multiple regions. It generates revenue by enabling clients to identify optimal antibodies and target-binding proteins for drug development using proprietary transgenic animal technologies and AI-driven tools.

The company's distinctive approach uses genetically modified animals—rats, chickens, mice, and cows—whose immune systems produce human-sequence antibodies. This biological intelligence strategy, combined with specialized platforms for bispecific and single-domain antibodies, differentiates its technology offerings.

company_profile.name: Optical Cable Corporation
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Optical Cable Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells fiber optic and copper data communications cabling and connectivity solutions primarily for the enterprise market in the United States and internationally. The company offers fiber optic and hybrid cables for high bandwidth transmission of data, video, and audio communications; and copper datacom cables, including unshielded and shielded twisted pair constructions. Its fiber optic connectivity products include fiber optic wall mounts, cabinet mount and rack mount enclosures, NEMA enclosures, pre-terminated fiber optic enclosures, fiber optic connectors, splice trays, fiber optic jumpers, plug and play cassette modules, pre-terminated fiber optic cable assemblies, adapters, and accessories; and copper connectivity products comprising category compliant patch panels, jacks, plugs, patch cords, faceplates, surface mounted boxes, distribution and multi-media boxes, copper rack mount and wall mount enclosures, cable assemblies, cable organizers, and other wiring products for equipment rooms, telecommunications closets, data centers, and workstation applications. In addition, the company provides network, data storage, and telecommunications management systems, such as data cabinets, wall mount enclosures, horizontal and vertical cable management systems, and open frame relay racks; and datacom wiring products, including various enclosures, and modules and modular outlets for single dwelling and multiple dwelling residential uses. Further, the company provides specialty fiber optic connectors and connectivity components, copper datacom connectors, and related systems and solutions for military, other harsh environment, and special applications, as well as cylindrical connector products. It sells its products to distributors, original equipment manufacturers, value-added resellers, and end-users. Optical Cable Corporation was incorporated in 1983 and is headquartered in Roanoke, Virginia.
company_profile.description_summary:
# Optical Cable Corporation

Manufactures and sells fiber optic and copper data communications cabling and connectivity solutions for enterprise markets globally. Revenue comes from fiber optic cables, copper datacom cables, and comprehensive connectivity products including enclosures, connectors, and patch panels for data centers, telecommunications closets, and workstations.

Distinctive offerings include specialty fiber optic connectors and connectivity components for military and harsh environment applications, plus network management systems and residential datacom wiring products serving diverse customer segments from distributors to end-users.

bull_points: Order backlog surged over 50% to $10.4M as of Q1 2026, with management citing increasing activity and quote requests in data center markets, suggesting near-term revenue acceleration.
Gross margin expanded to 32.7% in Q1 2026 from prior year, driven by production efficiencies and operating leverage, positioning the company for earnings accretion as volume grows.

bear_points: Cash on hand declined to just $126K as of Q1 2026 despite gross profit growth, raising concerns about working capital constraints and potential liquidity pressure if sales momentum stalls.

bull_points: Gross profit growth of 24.1% in fiscal 2025 against 9.5% revenue growth demonstrates strong operating leverage, suggesting the company can expand profitability substantially as it reaches higher volume thresholds.

Management explicitly identified data center expansion (Tier 2/3 MTDCs) and AI/cloud computing demand as growth catalysts for fiscal 2026 and beyond, with new loose-tube fiber cable products opening addressable market opportunities.

Financial strength score of 3/10 and current ratio of 1.82 provide a foundation for debt management and working capital flexibility to fund growth initiatives, while the company has already narrowed net losses by 64% year-over-year.
bear_points: Severe balance sheet constraints are evident: debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 9.14x and negative ROE of -3.93% indicate the company is overleveraged and destroying shareholder value, with limited capacity to invest in scale.

Profitability score of 0/10 and negative profit margin of -1% reflect a company still struggling to convert revenue growth into earnings; with base growth rate capped at 2% due to weak financial strength, long-term earnings power remains deeply uncertain.

With zero expected 5-year EPS growth despite 4.4% revenue growth, structural challenges in cost control and capital efficiency suggest the company lacks a sustainable competitive advantage to justify current valuation multiples.

company_profile.name: Ocugen, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Ocugen, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company, focuses on discovering, developing, and commercializing novel gene and cell therapies, biologic, and vaccines that improve patients' health. The company's pipeline product includes OCU400, a novel gene therapy product candidate restoring retinal integrity and function across a range of genetically diverse inherited retinal diseases, currently under Phase 3 trials for the treatment of retinitis pigmentosa and Phase 1/2 trials for the treatment of leber congenital amaurosis; OCU410, a gene therapy under phase 1/2 for the treatment of dry age-related macular degeneration (AMD); and OCU410ST, a gene therapy under phase 1/2 for the treatment of Stargardt disease. It is involved in the development of OCU200, a novel recombinant fusion protein for the treatment of diabetic macular edema, diabetic retinopathy, and wet age-related macular degeneration; and NeoCart, an autologous chondrocyte-derived neocartilage for the repair of knee cartilage injuries in adult. In addition, the company is developing OCU500, a COVID-19 vaccine; OCU510, a seasonal quadrivalent flu vaccine; and OCU520, a combination quadrivalent seasonal flu and COVID-19 vaccine. It has collaboration agreements with National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases for early clinical studies for the OCU500 program; and a strategic partnership with CanSino Biologics Inc. for manufacturing its modifier gene therapy pipeline product candidates. The company was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in Malvern, Pennsylvania.
company_profile.description_summary:
# Ocugen, Inc.

A biopharmaceutical company developing gene and cell therapies, biologics, and vaccines targeting inherited retinal diseases, age-related macular degeneration, diabetic eye conditions, knee cartilage injuries, and infectious diseases. It generates revenue through its pipeline of therapeutic candidates in various clinical trial phases.

The company distinguishes itself through specialized focus on ophthalmologic conditions and partnerships with the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases and CanSino Biologics for vaccine and gene therapy development.

_meta.type_rationale: Bank or thrift — P/Tangible Book prioritised; DCF excluded
_meta.method_explanation: Banks make money from the difference between what they charge on loans and what they pay on deposits — not from traditional business cash flows. company_profile.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 15.8%; DCF methods prioritised
company_profile.description: Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. operates as a less-than-truckload motor carrier in the United States and North America. The company offers regional, inter-regional, and national less-than-truckload services, as well as expedited transportation services. It also provides various value-added services, including container drayage, truckload brokerage, and supply chain consulting. In addition, the company operates fleet maintenance centers. As of December 31, 2025, it owned and operated 10,184 tractors, 30,824 linehaul trailers, and 14,313 pickup and delivery trailers. Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. was founded in 1934 and is headquartered in Thomasville, North Carolina.
company_profile.description_summary: Operates as a less-than-truckload motor carrier across the United States and North America, providing regional, inter-regional, and national transportation services. Generates primary revenue from freight shipping supplemented by expedited transport, container drayage, truckload brokerage, and supply chain consulting. Maintains an extensive fleet of over 10,000 tractors and 45,000 trailers supported by owned fleet maintenance centers.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 15.8%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

quality.note: Moat scored by Haiku API — final_score_pct computed out of 50.

quality_narrative.moat_rationale: Old Dominion's 23% return on equity and 21% return on invested capital, combined with an 18.5% profit margin, demonstrate pricing power and operational efficiency that competitors struggle to match—hallmarks of a strong competitive moat built on scale and execution excellence.

metric_assessments.profitability: Old Dominion converts 18.5 cents of every revenue dollar into profit, which is exceptional for a trucking company where typical margins hover around 5–10%. The company's 23% return on equity means it generates $0.23 in profit for every dollar of shareholder capital invested, far above the 10–15% that most industrial businesses achieve, showing it deploys capital with rare efficiency. The one constraint is that margins, while outstanding, are subject to cyclical freight demand and fuel costs, which can compress during downturns and prevent them from expanding further.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: With a debt-to-EBITDA ratio of just 0.02x, Old Dominion carries almost no financial leverage, and its 1.44 current ratio shows it has nearly $1.50 in liquid assets for every $1.00 of short-term obligations—both well above the safety thresholds most investors look for. This fortress balance sheet gives the company enormous flexibility to invest in equipment, weather downturns, or return cash to shareholders without financial stress. The only reason this is not a 10/10 is that the company could theoretically use modest leverage to amplify returns, so the pristine balance sheet, while a strength, also signals a slightly conservative capital structure.
metric_assessments.growth: Revenue declined 2.9% year-over-year, reflecting the cyclical weakness in freight markets and softer demand in the industrial economy, which is below the 2–3% long-term growth typical of mature transportation companies. Offsetting this near-term softness, the company is expected to deliver 13.4% annual earnings-per-share growth over the next five years, driven by operational leverage and margin expansion as freight markets normalize. Growth is held back by the cyclical nature of trucking and the fact that the industry is mature with limited runway for market share gains, meaning expansion depends heavily on the economy improving rather than the company capturing new opportunities.
metric_assessments.predictability: Old Dominion's long track record of consistent profitability and its established network of terminals across North America provide reasonable visibility into future performance, and the company's pricing power allows it to pass cost increases to customers with some reliability. However, the 6/10 score reflects the inherent unpredictability of freight volumes and economic cycles—when shipper demand drops, utilization falls and margins compress quickly, making quarter-to-quarter results harder to forecast with precision. The company's quality is predictable, but the magnitude of profits in any given period remains vulnerable to macroeconomic shocks.
metric_assessments.valuation: At current levels, Old Dominion's price does not offer much margin of safety for new investors; the stock is priced to reflect its excellent profitability and fortress balance sheet, leaving limited room for valuation multiple expansion if earnings simply grow at the expected 13.4% annual clip. A beginner buying today is essentially paying a full or premium price for quality and will rely on the company executing its growth guidance to earn an adequate return. For investors seeking a bargain entry point, this is not one—you are buying a best-in-class business at a best-in-class price.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Old Dominion earned a Safe rating because it combines fortress-like financial strength (nearly debt-free, strong liquidity) with exceptional and consistent profitability (23% ROE, 18.5% margins), meaning the business is unlikely to surprise investors with financial distress or earnings collapse. The main trade-off is that the stock is not cheap—you pay a premium valuation for this quality and safety, and growth is modest and cyclical, so returns will depend on the company executing in a normal or improving freight environment rather than on multiple expansion or breakout growth.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. demonstrates strong fundamental quality and scores highly across profitability, financial strength, and predictability, supported by a 20.57% ROIC. The risk profile is relatively contained with no major red flags across leverage, liquidity, or earnings consistency. With 13.4% consensus EPS growth forecast, the growth outlook supports the investment case. The stock trades at a 16% premium to estimated intrinsic value and is suitable for quality-focused long-term investors.

company_profile.description: Universal Display Corporation engages in the research, development, and commercialization of organic light emitting diode (OLED) technologies and materials for use in display and solid-state lighting applications. It offers phosphorescent organic light-emitting diode (PHOLED) technologies and materials for displays and lighting products under the UniversalPHOLED brand. The company also offers FOLED that are flexible OLEDs for the fabrication of OLEDs on flexible substrates; and OVJP, an organic vapor jet printing technology. In addition, it provides technology development and support services, including third-party collaboration and support to third parties for the commercialization of their OLED products; and contract research services in the areas of chemical synthesis research, development, and commercialization for non-OLED applications, as well as engages in the intellectual property and technology licensing activities. The company has operations in South Korea, China, Japan, the United States, and internationally. Universal Display Corporation was incorporated in 1985 and is headquartered in Ewing, New Jersey.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company researches, develops, and commercializes organic light-emitting diode (OLED) technologies and materials for displays and lighting. It generates revenue through phosphorescent OLED technologies, flexible OLED solutions, organic vapor jet printing technology, and licensing intellectual property to manufacturers worldwide.

The company distinguishes itself by offering comprehensive technology support services and contract research, enabling third parties to commercialize OLED products while maintaining a strong patent portfolio across multiple markets.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 20.6%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

company_profile.name: Okta, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Okta, Inc. operates as an identity partner in the United States and internationally. It offers Single Sign-on to secure access to cloud and on-premises applications from any device; Adaptive MFA for a risk-based layer of security for an organization's cloud, mobile, and web applications; API Access Management, which enables organizations to secure APIs as systems; Access Gateway, which extends the Okta platform to hybrid IT environments; Okta Device Access, which extends Okta platform's secure access management to the device login experience; Universal Directory for a cloud-based system of record. The company also provides Identity Threat Protection; Identity Security Posture Management for security measures and safeguards digital assets; Okta for AI Agents to discover, register, authenticate, govern, and manage AI Agents; Identity Governance and Administration products, including Lifecycle Management, Okta Workflows, Okta Identity Governance, and Cross App Access; Advanced Server Access for continuous and contextual access management to secure cloud infrastructure; and Okta Privileged Access to reduce risk with unified access and governance management. In addition, it provides Universal Login, a standards-based login infrastructure; Attack Protection Suite to minimize risks associated with identity-targeted attacks; Adaptive MFA; Passwordless, which enables users to login without a password; Machine-to-Machine Tokens for authentication and authorization with NHIs; Private Cloud, a deployment option; Organizations, which support a large number of partners or customers; Extensibility, which enables customers to build customized identity flows; Fine Grained Authorization, which manages complex authorization scenarios; and Auth0 for AI Agents to secure and scale agentic applications. The company was formerly known as Saasure, Inc. Okta, Inc. was incorporated in 2009 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
Okta provides identity and access management solutions, enabling secure authentication and authorization across cloud and on-premises applications. Its platform offers single sign-on, adaptive multi-factor authentication, API access management, and identity governance tools, generating revenue through these comprehensive security solutions.

The company distinctly serves hybrid IT environments and emerging needs like AI agent authentication, while offering deployment flexibility through private cloud options and extensibility features that let customers build customized identity workflows.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

quality.note: Moat scored by Haiku API — final_score_pct computed out of 50.

quality_narrative.moat_rationale: Okta has a moderate moat anchored in switching costs and predictable recurring revenue, but weak returns on invested capital and slowing growth limit its competitive defensibility.

metric_assessments.profitability: An 8.2% profit margin is below the 12–15% typical of healthy software companies, and a 3.67% return on equity trails the 15% benchmark for strong performers. The company is extracting reasonable profits from its sales, but not at the efficiency level that signals pricing power or operational dominance—the limiting factor is that most of the revenue growth is being consumed by rising costs rather than flowing to the bottom line.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: A current ratio of 1.43 and debt-to-EBITDA of 1.54 are both solid metrics that sit comfortably in the safe zone for a software business. The balance sheet has room to invest or weather downturns without distress. However, the company is not fortress-like; the debt load is moderate rather than negligible, so future earnings hiccups could matter more than they would for a fully unlevered competitor.
metric_assessments.growth: Revenue is expanding at 11.2% annually and earnings are forecast to grow at a similar 11.2% over the next five years—both respectable absolute rates but well below the 20–30% growth typical of high-growth software peers. This slowdown is the core weakness: while the business is not shrinking, it has transitioned from a high-growth narrative to a mature growth profile, which typically commands lower valuations and attracts fewer growth-focused investors.
metric_assessments.predictability: A 6/10 score reflects a software-as-a-service model with recurring subscription revenue, which is inherently sticky and predictable; however, the company's own earnings power is dampened by the margin pressure noted above, making the path from revenue growth to profit growth less clean than it should be. Strong revenue visibility is offset by execution risk on cost control, preventing a higher score.
metric_assessments.valuation: A 3/10 valuation score signals the stock is not cheap relative to its fundamentals; with 11% growth and 8% margins, Okta is priced for a company bigger than it has become. The market appears to be pricing in either a return to faster growth or margin expansion that has not materialized, leaving little margin of safety for new buyers.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Okta is classified as Speculative because it combines decent but slowing growth, weak returns on capital, and a stretched valuation that leaves little room for disappointment. The business is profitable and financially sound, but it is no longer expanding fast enough to justify a premium price, and the returns it generates on shareholder money are below-average, making it a higher-risk bet than a mature, profitable business trading at a fair multiple.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Okta is a profitable software-as-a-service company with a strong balance sheet and sticky customer relationships that support predictable revenue, but it has matured into 11% annual growth—typical of an established business, not a high-flyer—while generating only 3.67% returns on equity, well below what investors expect from technology leaders. The core risk is that the stock's current price assumes either a return to faster growth or significant margin improvement, neither of which the company has demonstrated recently, leaving investors exposed to disappointment and further re-rating lower. Growth is constrained by the competitive landscape and market saturation in identity management, and profitability margins are being squeezed by rising operating costs. At its current valuation, the risk-reward is unfavorable: you are paying a premium price for a business delivering below-premium growth and returns, which is why the stock lands in speculative territory. Unless you see a clear catalyst for accelerating growth or margin recovery, this is a stock to watch rather than chase.

company_profile.description: Olema Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the discovery, development, and commercialization of therapies for women's cancers. Olema Pharmaceuticals, Inc. was incorporated in 2006 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (energy) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

_meta.type_rationale: Bank or thrift — P/Tangible Book prioritised; DCF excluded
_meta.method_explanation: Banks make money from the difference between what they charge on loans and what they pay on deposits — not from traditional business cash flows. Because of this, standard cash flow models (DCF) do not work well for banks. Instead, we focus on Price-to-Book (P/B), which compares the stock price to the value of the bank's assets on its books. Banks also carry a lot of debt by design — they borrow money to lend it out — so high leverage is normal and not a red flag.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

The company was formerly known as BeiGene, Ltd. and changed its name to BeOne Medicines AG in May 2025. BeOne Medicines AG was founded in 2010 and is based in Basel, Switzerland.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 16.0%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.
